metadata.report_title: 🔬 Savage Command Benchmarker Report - Scientific Roasting Results
metadata.analyst: SAVAGE COMMAND BENCHMARKER with PhD in roasting bad code
metadata.methodology: Multi-dimensional performance analysis with brutal statistical honesty

executive_summary.overall_verdict: 💥 Your command arsenal has serious quality control issues
executive_summary.savage_summary: With a mean quality of 3.85/10, your commands are performing like a broken calculator - technically functional but nobody wants to rely on them. The standard deviation of 1.92 shows you're not just consistently bad, you're inconsistently bad.

poor_2_to_4.comment: Matching mediocrity in the race to the bottom

complexity_vs_quality.savage_insight: Every complexity violation you add is like kicking quality in the teeth. This -0.935 correlation is so strong it's practically a law of physics in your codebase.

usability_vs_quality.savage_insight: Usable commands tend to be better quality. Revolutionary insight right there.

ultrathink-pure-xml.md.savage_verdict: 🏆 The sole survivor in this quality wasteland. Actually follows CLAUDE.md principles. Still has maintenance issues but compared to the others, it's basically Shakespeare.

adaptive-complexity-router.md.savage_verdict: 💥 A complexity router that's too complex to route complexity. The irony is so thick you could cut it with a knife. Maintenance burden score: 10/10 - future developers will curse your name.

adhd-context-switch.md.savage_verdict: 😬 Low complexity but terrible UX. It's like building a simple door that nobody can figure out how to open. The code archaeology score of 7 means future devs will need Indiana Jones to maintain this.

context-enhanced-executor.md.savage_verdict: 😐 Perfect usability wasted on maximum maintenance burden. It's like putting a beautiful interface on a ticking time bomb. Users love it until they have to fix it.

generate-thinking-command.md.savage_verdict: 💥 A command generator that violates its own quality standards. Factory pattern addiction (7/10) combined with maximum maintenance burden. It's like a diet book written by someone who's never seen a vegetable.

safe-code-beautifier.md.savage_verdict: 🔥 BURN IT WITH FIRE. A 'safe' beautifier that's about as safe as juggling chainsaws. Complexity 7/10, terrible UX (3/10), and maximum maintenance burden. The only beautiful thing about this command would be watching it get deleted.

immediate_actions.evidence: Overall score 1.06/10 with maximum scores in all negative metrics
immediate_actions.savage_rationale: This command is beyond salvation. It's like trying to polish a turd - you'll just get your hands dirty.

immediate_actions.evidence: Both show factory pattern addiction (7/10) and maximum maintenance burden
immediate_actions.savage_rationale: These commands are examples of what happens when you let architects run wild without adult supervision.

immediate_actions.evidence: Low usability score (4/10) despite simple implementation
immediate_actions.savage_rationale: Simple but unusable is like having a race car with square wheels.

strategic_improvements.rationale: Mean quality 3.85/10 is below acceptable threshold
strategic_improvements.specific_metrics: Reject any command with complexity_score > 5 or maintenance_burden > 7

strategic_improvements.rationale: Strong negative correlation (-0.935) between complexity and quality
strategic_improvements.specific_metrics: Maximum 5 XML nesting levels, complexity score cap at 3

strategic_improvements.rationale: 33.3% of commands have poor usability scores
strategic_improvements.specific_metrics: Minimum 3 usage examples, clear parameter documentation

sample_validity.bias_assessment: Low selection bias, representative of command variety

measurement_reliability.metric_consistency: High - all metrics show logical relationships
measurement_reliability.face_validity: High - results match subjective code quality assessment

savage_final_verdict.overall_assessment: Your command arsenal has the quality consistency of a random number generator. One decent command (ultrathink-pure-xml) carries the entire sample while the rest range from mediocre to catastrophically bad.
savage_final_verdict.harshest_truth: With 66.7% high maintenance risk and a mean quality of 3.85/10, your commands are like a code review nightmare waiting to happen. The safe-code-beautifier.md scoring 1.06/10 is so bad it's actually impressive - it takes real skill to violate every quality principle simultaneously.
savage_final_verdict.constructive_brutality: The -0.935 correlation between complexity and quality isn't just a statistic - it's a mathematical proof that you're doing complexity wrong. Every abstraction layer you add is like adding another chain to the anchor dragging your code quality to the bottom.
savage_final_verdict.call_to_action: Delete the worst offender, refactor the factory pattern addicts, and implement quality gates before your command system becomes a cautionary tale in software engineering textbooks.

methodology_notes.metrics_justification: Each metric targets specific CLAUDE.md violations and real-world usability factors
methodology_notes.scoring_calibration: Thresholds based on industry standards and CLAUDE.md complexity rules
methodology_notes.statistical_approach: 95% confidence intervals, correlation analysis, risk stratification
methodology_notes.limitations: Sample size of 6 provides moderate statistical power. Full population analysis recommended for definitive conclusions.
